Narrative: On Saturday, January 4th, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report of a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I met the registered owner, who reported that his vehicle was parked in the driveway at 21:30 hours on 01/03/2025. He further reported that he left his residence with his friends in his friend's vehicle and returned to his residence on the morning of 01/04/2025. He noticed his vehicle was taken from his driveway. The registered owner reported there was nothing valuable in the car and was uncertain if the spare keys were in the vehicle. Both New Jersey plates are affixed to the vehicle.      Communications advised me that the vehicle was located in Clarkstown, NY, and was involved in a motor vehicle hit-and-run accident. The registered owner was also advised of the vehicle's location. I observed no cameras in the area.      The victim did not want the vehicle processed or to sign complaints if the suspect(s) are identified.     A New Jersey Victim Notification form was completed and provided to the registered owner.     An ATTF entry was completed    A motor vehicle checklist was completed and submitted.

Narrative: On Friday January 10, 2025, I was assigned as the Hackensack High School SRO. I was advised that a student wanted to report a crime and was currently in the main office. Upon arriving to the main office I met the victim, A.R.       As I began to speak with the victim she immediately began to cry. She stated that she was inappropriately touched by the suspect who is also student. The suspect was identified as Manuel Martinez Perez. The victim stated that on Tuesday January 7, 2025  she was in her 9th period class ESL 2 in room 276. She shares this class with the suspect and his cousin Esmeralda Perez Martinez who is a potential witness.       The victim stated during class the suspect gave her a hug. As he was hugging her he dropped his hand down and grabbed her breast over her clothing. She immediately became angry and told him not to touch her. She then advised Esmeralda Perez Martinez that she was touched inappropriately by the suspect. The victim stated that she reported this incident to her her teacher Mrs. Magdalene Symmonds, but she did not understand her due to a language barrier.  The victim reported that this was not the first time she was inappropriately touched by the suspect. She reported that the prior week a similar incident occurred. I notified assistant Principal Celso King of this report.      I spoke to Esmeralda Perez Martinez who may have witness the incident. Perez Martinez stated that she sits in a group with the victim and the suspect. The day of the incident she did not observe the suspect touch the victim because she was busy doing her class work. The witness did confirm that the victim mentioned to her that she was inappropriately touched by the suspect.       The victim's mother, Raquel Lelio, was notified of the incident and responded to the school. She advised me that she was not sure if she wanted to sign criminal complaints against the suspect. She wants to speak with her daughter and husband before making a decision.       Det. E. Reyes was advised of the incident and will follow up with the complainant at a later time.

Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown.     I responded to the listed location, 90 Prospect Avenue, on a report of a burglary to auto. Upon arrival, I was met by the victim, Kelly Nurnberg-Darzi, who stated on the morning of 01/10/25 she responded to her grey 2006 Honda Pilot and observed all of her work equipment to be missing. The victim advised me she specialized in opening safes and stores several of her high-value tools in the rear of the listed vehicle. The victim stated the listed vehicle was parked in assigned space #20 and did not move it from the assigned space. The victim stated she last recalled observing her tools in the listed vehicle on 01/06/25 and had not returned to the vehicle since. The following items were taken from the listed vehicle:2 Boroscope - $5,000.00 USD8 Redundant Locks - $2,000.00 USD5 Drills - $5,000.00 USD1 large box of drill bits - $1,200.00 USD1 tool bag with several tools - $2,000.00 USD6 Electronic locks - $1,320.00 USD4 FAS locks - $480.00 Total Value - $17,000.00 USD     While on scene, I observed several cameras in the parking garage of 90 Prospect Avenue. I further observed the listed vehicle to have a broken rear passenger side window which gives direct access to the vehicles trunk. The victim stated the damage occurred on 01/04/25 while her son was parking the vehicle. The victim further stated she did not have any time to repair the broken window and remove the tools from the vehicle prior to them being taken.      The victim advised me she believes the suspect may be her husband Uri Darzi whom she is currently in a civil lawsuit with. Sgt. Peralta responded assist.A victim notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim.

Narrative: On Monday, January 13th, 2025, at approximately 12:07 hours, P.O. Alvarez and I responded to the listed location on a dispute report. Before our arrival, headquarters had advised us that the male suspect, described as wearing a grey hoodie and sweatpants, had fled southbound on First Street. Upon arrival, we met with Maria Romero Peralta, the mother of the juvenile victim, A.R.      She reported that the described suspect, later identified as D.T., had punched her in the left eye and her right breast. With the mother's permission and in her presence, we spoke to the victim, a juvenile male, A.R., who reported that the suspect, D.T., had stolen a red electronic moped bike and had dropped it off at his house. He further reported that the suspect asked the victim if he could drop off the stolen moped for a couple of days. The victim also reported giving the stolen moped bike to another friend since he did not know how to operate the bike. A.R. later noted that the suspect, D.T., recovered the stolen moped bike from the said friend they have in common and had the bike confiscated byLaw Enforcement in another jurisdiction.      Today, the victim reported that the suspect arrived at his residence demanding $400.00 for the moped because it had been confiscated. He further reported that the suspect arrived with three (3) other friends: two Hispanic males, unknown names, dates of birth, or addresses, and a Hispanic female, blonde hair and blue jacket, operating a Toyota Rav 4 with an unknown NY tag. He also reported that a fight ensued between him and the suspect, and the three (3) witnesses stood by.      The victim also reported that his mother, Maria Romero Peralta, heard the commotion and broke up the fight, retreating with the victim and herself back into the residence. A.R. reported that the front entrance door closed and was being held by his mother when the suspect breached the door with physical force and continued to assault the victim while demanding $400.00 for the red moped electric bike.  I then requested a supervisor, and Sgt. Williams responded to assist.     Maria Romero Peralta reported that while attempting to break up the fight, she was punched in the left eye and right breast by the suspect, D.T. She also reported that the suspect and the three (3) witnesses fled the scene after she threatened to call the police. Maria Romero Peralta, along with A.R. declined medical attention at the scene. The mother and the victim wanted to provide statements to detectives and were transported to headquarters by P.O. Alvarez as I followed.      At headquarters, Detectives Mora and Garrett responded to report room 2 and met with the victim and his mother. Before providing statements to the detectives, Maria Romero Peralta began to have complaints of pain in her left eye and right breast. I observed her left eye to be swollen and red. Maria Romero Peralta then requested to be transported to the hospital for evaluation and treatment. I requested an ambulance, and Hackensack University Medical Center BLS #136 arrived. The mother, now a patient, was transported to the HUMC along with A.R. for treatment. The patient reported that she would respond to headquarters at a later time to provide statements to the detectives. I contacted DCP&P Christine #1718 and advised of the incident. She reported that the incident would only be documented. I completed a victim notification form and provided a copy to the victim.It should be noted that the victim, A.R., reported that he had dropped out of school as of today; therefore, a handle with care was not completed. See Detectives report for additional information.

Narrative: On Tuesday, January 14th, 2025, P.O. Conlon. P.O. Voorhis, and I responded to 450 Hackensack Avenue for a report of a trespasser on the premises who had shoplifted in the past and has also been aggressive with staff in the past.    Upon my arrival, I observed the arrestee enter a white Volkswagon (NJ-Y82VDS) that was leaving the parking lot. The security officers at Home Depot also pointed out the arrestee as he entered the car. I conducted a motor vehicle stop with the vehicle at the traffic light exiting the parking lot. As I approached the vehicle, the arrestee exited the vehicle and started running West on Hackensack Avenue. P.O. Conlon, P.O. Voorhis, and I began running after the arrestee and announcing ourselves as Hackensack Police. When the arrestee approached the other side of Hackensack Avenue, he stopped, and P.O. Conlon apprehended him. The arrestee was flailing his arms while I was attempting to place him in handcuffs. The arrestee was then put in handcuffs with his hands behind his back. The arrestee was then searched, which yielded negative for contraband.   The Loss Prevention Officers stated that the arrestee entered the front door of Home Depot with a black ski covering his face. The loss prevention officers noticed the arrestee from previous shoplifting. The arrestee then concealed several power tools totaling $1,284.00 and placed them in a shopping cart. The arrestee then went past all points of sale and tried to exit the store. Prior to exiting the store, the loss prevention officers approached him and tried to stop him from leaving. The arrestee then pushed the loss prevention officers and fled the store on foot. The loss prevention officer recovered the items. The arrestee then entered the listed vehicle and fled the parking lot for the exit of the mall. A photo copy of the receipt was placed into the BCI mailbox located at the front at the front desk.  The items that were recovered: M18 Jobsite Radio priced at $189.00Two 20V Atomic BL Combo Kit priced at $149.00 eachM18 Fuel Jig Saw priced at $199.00MKE M12 Packout Flood Light priced at $149.00M18 Fuel 10 Miter Saw priced at $449.00     Totaling at $1,284.00.The arrestee was placed in radio car 114 and transported to police headquarters for processing. The vehicle was checked before and after the transport for contraband which yielded negative   The arrestee was processed at police headquarters by P.O. Caero. Complaint W-2025-000016 was authored by Lt. Swetits against the arrestee and was signed for 2C:29-2a(1), 2C:29-2a(2), and two counts of 2C:15-1A(1).  The arrestee also had multiple outstanding warrants from various jurisdictions.The arrestee was remanded to the Bergen County Jail.

Narrative: On 01/15/2025 I responded to 244 First Street on a reported motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival I met with the driver of the stolen vehicle, Jeffrey Mejiamorquecho. He reported he parked his motor vehicle in front of 244 First Street facing north at around 24:00 hours. He further reported his mother left his residence at 05:00 hours on the listed date and noticed the vehicle was gone. The driver also reported he received a notification from EZ Pass with a charge from the George Washington Bridge toll at 04:13 hours on the listed date. The driver then walked out with me to where his vehicle was parked and observed broken glass which he suspected to be from his rear passenger side window. The driver advised me the vehicle had 1 plate in the rear and was worth approximately $50,000.00 as the vehicle was fairly new. He further reported he attempted to track the vehicle as it contains tracking equipment but was unsuccessful as it was not connecting. He believes it was disconnected somehow after the vehicle was stolen.240 First Street has cameras in the general direction of where the vehicle was parked. It is unknown at this time if they captured the incident occur.Insurance Information: Company: Geico (369)Policy Number: 6183163036The driver was provided with a completed New Jersey Victim Notification Form.The driver wished to have the vehicle processed if located and a Hackensack Police Consent to Search from was completed.The vehicle was entered into NCIC as stolen by Communications.The vehicle was entered into ATTF as stolen.

Narrative: On Thursday, January 16, 2025 at approximately 1934 hours, Sgt. Peralta and I responded to 30 Second Street, Apt. L1 on report of a residential burglary. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported that she left her apartment at approximately 1200 hours on this date, before returning several hours later, at approximately 1800 hours. The victim further reported that upon returning to her apartment, she observed the exterior door knob to be damaged (loose), as if someone attempted to break in. The victim stated that upon entering the apartment, the interior did not appear to be ransacked, nor did anything appear out of place at this time.      The scene was unable to processed due to the victim contaminating evidence while entering her apartment. The cost of repair for the damaged door knob was approximately $35.00. A Victim Notification was completed and the victim was provided with a copy upon our departure. There were no known suspects at the time this report was taken. Det./Sgt. Tripodi was notified.

Narrative: Suspect 1: Hispanic male, approximately early 30' s in age, approximately 5'10" in height, approximately 180 in weight, wearing a tan hooded sweater, brown "NY" Yankees logo hat, black sweatpants and white sneakers.Suspect 2: Hispanic female, approximately mid 20's in age, approximately 5'5" in height, approximately 140 in weight, wearing a black jacket, grey hooded sweater, tan pants and cream colored shoes. Suspect Vehicle: 4 door Silver in color Hyundai Sonata (unknown registration).On 1/17/25 P.O. Papanikolaou and I responded to 630 Main Street, Target on a reported shoplifting. While   speaking with Target Loss Prevention they wanted to report an additional incident, which occurred in the past.Target Loss Prevention / victim, Cheikh Gaye, reported on 1/16/25 at approximately 21:03 hours suspect 1 and suspect 2 entered Target. It was further reported on 1/16/25 at approximately 21:16 hours suspect 1 and 2 passed all points of sale with unpaid merchandise in a shopping cart. The unpaid merchandise (furniture, grocery, health and beauty, home and kitchen products) totaled $452.71. Prior to exiting Target Loss Prevention / the victim confronted both suspects. Target Loss Prevention were able to recover all merchandise that both suspects attempted to steal. While recovering the stolen merchandise suspect 1 punched the victim in the head, specifically the top left side, with a closed fist one time. The victim reported having a complaint of pain hours after being assaulted. Both suspect later left the area in the suspect vehicle. A Sgt. was requested and Sgt. Competiello responded. Detectives were notified. The victim later responded to headquarters to provide a voluntary statement. The victim refused medical attention at this time.A receipt of the unpaid merchandise was provided by Target Loss Prevention. The receipt was later secured in the Detective Bureau Mailbox.

Narrative: On January 22, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report on a stolen motor vehicle. Upon arrival, I met with the registered owner, Kamil Garcia. The registered owner stated on January 21, 2025 at approximately 1800 hours he parked his vehicle, a 2023 Honda Passport color red bearing NJ registration U22RSV in front of 33 Ross Ave facing West. The registered owner stated that he currently has possession of the two key fobs that belong to the vehicle. At approximately 0309 hours on January 22, 2025 he responded to his vehicle where he last parked it and the vehicle was no longer there.       The registered owner received a completed victim notification. The vehicle was entered as stolen into NCIC (NIC #  V956704006) along with a stolen motor vehicle form. The vehicle was also entered as stolen into the  ATTF website. The registered owner does not wish to have the vehicle processed at this time. Approximate value for the above vehicle is 46,000.00 USD.

Narrative: On January 22, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report of a stolen motor vehicle. Upon arrival, I met with the victim, Melisssa Williams, also registered owner. The victim stated the vehicle has been lodged at 443 Hudson Street with permission of the resident Jermal Clark. The victim stated upon speaking to Jermal Clark he stated "the vehicle was towed off the property early morning and he got some money for it."  Sgt. Tovbin and I spoke to Jermal Clark, who stated that he does not know which towing company towed the vehicle. I asked Jermal Clark if he towed the vehicle off the property and he was uncooperative in answering the question.       The vehicle, a 2006 Nissan Pathfinder color black bearing NJ registration Y62SRJ, was last seen on January 21, 2025 at approximately 1600 hours parked facing South on the listed property. The victim stated that she currently does not have possession of the key. The victim indicated the one key was last in possession of Jermal Clark.       The registered owner received a completed victim notification. The vehicle was entered as stolen into NCIC (NIC # V146697789 ) along with a stolen motor vehicle form. The vehicle was also entered as stolen into the  ATTF website. The registered owner does not wish to have the vehicle processed at this time. Approximate value for the above vehicle is 2,500.00 USD.

The victim stated upon speaking to Jermal Clark he stated "the vehicle was towed off the property early morning and he got some money for it."  Sgt. Tovbin and I spoke to Jermal Clark, who stated that he does not know which towing company towed the vehicle. I asked Jermal Clark if he towed the vehicle off the property and he was uncooperative in answering the question.       The vehicle, a 2006 Nissan Pathfinder color black bearing NJ registration Y62SRJ, was last seen on January 21, 2025 at approximately 1600 hours parked facing South on the listed property. The victim stated that she currently does not have possession of the key. The victim indicated the one key was last in possession of Jermal Clark.       The registered owner received a completed victim notification. The vehicle was entered as stolen into NCIC (NIC # V146697789 ) along with a stolen motor vehicle form. The vehicle was also entered as stolen into the  ATTF website. The registered owner does not wish to have the vehicle processed at this time. Approximate value for the above vehicle is 2,500.00 USD.

Narrative: On Thursday, 01/23/2025 I was notified by D/Sgt. Dominguez of a possible sexual assault involving a juvenile. I was advised that 10 y/o S.S. disclosed to a school teacher that her mothers boyfriend has been touching her while her mother was sleeping. This reportedly has occurred on multiple occasions. No further information was available at this time. S.S. is a student at Nelly K. Parker school, who already notified DCP&P as per protocol.    I contacted DCP&P social worker Jennifer Diaz who was assigned to the initial investigation. Ms. Diaz advised me that she was currently enroute to Nelly K. Parker to meet with S.S., and school staff. She advised me that she already contacted BCPO/SVU. I advised her that I will meet her at the school. Upon arrival I met with Ms. Diaz, who advised me that S.S. was set to be picked up from school by another unknown adult possibly the suspect. Due to the circumstances it was determined that S.S. will be transported to BCPO (100 Eisenhower Drive, Paramus NJ) to undergo a forensic interview with SVU. Contact was made to S.S. mother, Ibys Sandoval, who was advised that a police investigation is underway and her presence is required. Ms. Sandoval expressed that she has transportation issues, therefore this agency arranged transport for her.  Det. Reyes responded to pick up Ms. Sandoval at her residence (176 Berdan Place, Hackensack NJ). Ms. Sandoval was transported to Nelly K. Parker to meet up with her daughter, and ultimately transported to BCPO for the interview.   Contact information was limited at the time of this report (See Det. Reyes Supplemental report for further details)

Narrative: On January 25, 2025, at approximately 13:55 hours, P.O. P. Lopez, P.O. G. Lara-Nunez, P.O. Mattalian, Sgt. Competiello, and I responded to 60 Kansas apartment 8F for a possible burglary in progress. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported she left her apartment at approximately 11:30 hours. The victim reported approximately 14:00 hours, she returned to the apartment to find the front door slightly ajar.     We did a check of the interior of the apartment which yielded negative results for any suspects inside. The victim reported she is 100% sure the door was locked because she checked multiple times before leaving. The victim reported she was missing about $2,000 in miscellaneous jewelry. She described the jewelry to be earrings and gold necklaces.The victim reported she had the same incident occur in 2023. See 23-091001 for more. The victim reported she observed a light skin Hispanic male driving a black Lexus leaving the apartment complex as she pulled in. The victim reported she recalls the same Lexus and same male in the area after the previous incident.Sgt. Competiello contacted detectives. Detective DeBonis arrived on scene.The victim requested for the apartment to be processed. Sgt. Competiello contacted Bergen County Sheriff's Office. Detective LaSala #1866 arrived on scene. See BCSO case #2025-0052 for more .I observed no signs of forced entry. The New Jersey victim notification form was filled out and a copy was provided to the victim for her records.The crime scene was secured at 14:15 hours.Crime Scene Log:14:31: Det. LaSala Enters14:38: Det. LaSala Exits14:48: Det. Lasala Enters14:49: Det. Debonis Enters15:01: Det. LaSala Exits.15:04: Det. LaSala Enters.Crime Scene Terminated: 15:08

Narrative: Suspect description: Possibly male, approximately 5'7'' in height, with a slim build. On 1/26/25 P.O. Papanikolaou and I responded to 206 Stanley Place on a reported theft from auto. Upon arrival, I spoke with the victim, Phyllis Cunningham. The victim reported on 1/25/25 at approximately 20:52 hours she received a text message from her son. The text was a video of an unknown suspect entering her vehicle, New Jersey Registration R36GBB. The listed vehicle was parked in the driveway at 206 Stanley Place. The victim showed me the video that her son texted her. Upon observing the video I observed on 1/25/25 at 18:27 hours the suspect entered the vehicle from the front driver side door, which was left insecure. Later, the suspect was seen on video to be searching through the vehicle. After the incident the victim responded to check her vehicle. Upon inspecting her vehicle nothing appeared to be stolen and no damage was observed. A New Jersey victim notification form was completed. The victim did not request to have the vehicle processed. At this time the victim reported if the suspect could be identified she would pursue a criminal case.  The victim was advised to save the video surveillance of the incident. Sgt. Competiello was advised.

Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a reported theft from a motor vehicle. Sgt. Farhi responded to assist. Upon arrival, I spoke with the complainant. She stated that on 01/26/2025 at approximately 20:10 hours, she had turned her vehicle on to heat up. She further stated that she had left the vehicle running with the keys inside when she returned to her apartment for a few moments. The complainant reported that when she returned to the vehicle, the vehicle was still there but the keys were missing.       The complainant stated that no other items appeared to be missing from the vehicle. Furthermore, due to contamination by the complainant, the vehicle was unable to be processed.    The complainant stated that the value of the key fob is approximately $200.00.    The vehicle was parked on Conklin Place, 150 feet east of Pangborn Place, facing west.    A check of the area for cameras revealed one on the Bergen Christian Academies.       A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and provided to the complainant. She was further advised on how to obtain a copy of this report.  Detective Garrett was notified.Vehicle Insurance: GeicoCode :365Policy Number: 6164-87-88-42

Narrative: On January 29, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report on a stolen motor vehicle. Upon arrival, I met with the registered owner, Trina Coley. The registered owner stated on January 28, 2025 during the afternoon hours she parked her vehicle , a 2012 Lexus ES color black bearing NJ registration K84CXU in her residence private parking lot facing East. The registered owner stated that she currently does not have possession of the key fob as it was left inside of the vehicle along with her cell phone. At approximately 0930 hours on January 29, 2025 she responded to her vehicle where she last parked it,and the vehicle was no longer there.       The registered owner received a completed victim notification. The vehicle was entered as stolen into NCIC (NIC #V686695547 ) along with a stolen motor vehicle form. The vehicle was also entered as stolen into the ATTF web site. The registered owner does not wish to have the vehicle processed at this time. Approximate value for the above vehicle is 7,000.00 USD and the iPhone 15 at $800.00 USD.

Narrative: On January 31, 2025, at approximately 1200 hours the victim responded to Hackensack Police Department Headquarters for a report of a burglary to his residence that occurred in the past. I met with the victim who reported that last Friday, January 24, 2025 at approximately 1200 hours he left his residence with his wife and son for the day. He reported that upon his return to his residence at approximately 1500 hours, he observed the front door (allows entry into the apartment) wide open.  He further reported that he believes he locked the door before he left but did not observe any damage to his door. He reported that he went inside his residence to check if anyone was in his apartment with negative results. The victim reported that the following day, Saturday, January 25, 2025, he looked for his beige folder that contained copies and originals of the following items: passport, social security cards, and licenses of his wife, Arelis Bonilla, and his sons, Eduardo M. Sobrino and Edwin D. Sobrino-Bonilla.       The victim reported that he last saw his beige folder with those documents in his living room approximately one month ago. The victim was advised to contact the social security administration, DMV, and credit companies to advise them of the above. He was also advised on how to obtain new copies of his social security card, license, and passport. The victim was advised to report any fraudulent activity he may see.       A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and a copy was provided to the victim. He was advised to contact Hackensack Police Department should another incident like this arise.       The victim was not offered B.C.I. to respond to the residence due to the apartment being contaminated for approximately a week. Det. Lt. Weber was notified.

Narrative: On February 1, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report on a stolen motor vehicle. Upon arrival, I met with the registered owner, Mohammed Almowail. The registered owner stated on February 1, 2025 at approximately 0100 hours he parked his vehicle, a 2012 Ford Crown Victoria color grey bearing NJ registration P77UZJ in front of 453 Sutton Avenue facing East. At approximately 0230 hours, while inside of the residence he heard the vehicle being turned on and when he walked out to where he last parked it the vehicle was no longer there.      The registered owner stated that he currently has possession of the two keys that belong to the vehicle. The vehicle has two NJ plates(P77UZJ) attached.     It should be noted the victim was in the process of selling the vehicle today to someone by the alias of Taylor Jacobs via Facebook marketplace. The victim provided the above listed address and videos of where the vehicle was going to be located to the above listed alias of Taylor Jacobs.     The vehicle also contained a black wallet inside with three Bank of America Credit Cards and 4 Bank of Kuwaitt Credit Cards issued to Abdull Alhajeri. The victim was advised to report and lock all cards.      The registered owner received a completed victim notification. The vehicle was entered as stolen into NCIC (NIC # V786677145 ) along with a stolen motor vehicle form. The vehicle was also entered as stolen into the  ATTF site. The registered owner does not wish to have the vehicle processed at this time. Approximate value for the above vehicle is 3,000.00 USD. The value of the black wallet is approximately $30.00 USD.

Narrative: Unknown Suspect Descriptions:Unknown Suspect 1: Tall white male, crew cut, no clothing descriptionUnknown Suspects 2-5: 4 males, unknown race, wearing dark colored helmets and dark clothes with padding.         While in the area, P.O. Maceri and I were flagged down by the victim. Upon speaking with him, he advised that he had been robbed. Sgt. Farhi responded to assist.          The victim stated that on 02/02/2025 between 17:25 and 18:00 hours, he was walking north on Main Street, near the area of Main Street and Court Street with two acquaintances. He further stated that while walking in that area, a verbal dispute took place between the three of them. The victim reported that at this time, he was approached by five suspects, with four of them carrying bats. He further reported that suspect #1 threatened him, stating that he would beat him up if he didn't hand over his belongings. The victim stated that suspect #1 forcibly retrieved the wallet off his person and removed a debit card and identification card, before throwing the wallet back in his direction. He further stated that the suspect's then told him if they saw him again, they would beat him up. The suspects then fled east on Court Street, before turning left to head north on River Road.        A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and provided to the victim.       The detective division was notified and advised that they would follow up with the victim.

Narrative: On Monday, February 3, 2025 at approximately 1640 hours, Sgt. Farhi and I responded to 400 Hackensack Avenue (Marc Jacobs) on a reported robbery. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported that three suspects (1 male, 2 females) entered the establishment at approximately 1600 hours. The suspects reportedly exited and reentered the store multiples time prior to committing the offense, with the last entry being made only by the male suspect.     The victim further reported that the suspect grabbed three small purses from the display shelf before he began to walk toward the exit. At that time, the victim stated that he attempted to stop the suspect, who immediately shoved him into a store fixture. The suspect reportedly proceeded to run out of the store toward the nearest mall exit, where he entered a black vehicle and fled in an unknown direction. The victim stated that he was able to recover one of the purses during the incident.     The victim advised that he sustained minor injuries to his left arm as result of the incident, in which he refused any medical attention. The victim wished to provide a recorded statement to detectives. He was transported to police headquarters where he was turned over to Det. Dogali. The total loss suffered by the establishment for the two purses was approximately $650.00. The store manager (witness) wished to have complaints signed against the suspects if they were able to be identified.     Suspect 1: Black male, slim build, 5'8", black beanie, black puffer, dark pants, white/blue sneakers     Suspect 2: Black female, heavy build, 5'8", short hair, black puffer, black leggings, black pocketbook     Suspect 3: Black female, heavy build, 5'7", long cornrows, gray puffer, light blue jeans

Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a reported motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I spoke with the complainant. She stated that on 02/04/2025 at 11:29 hours, she received a text from her boss, asking her to check on one of their U-Haul vehicles. She further stated that upon searching the parking lot, she could not locate the vehicle. The complainant reported that the vehicle had been rented out, so she contacted the contract holder to ensure the vehicle was dropped off. Upon verifying such and checking camera footage, the vehicle is seen on camera being dropped off by the customer. He is then approached by the suspect, where a brief verbal exchange is had. The customer is then seen leaving the area. The suspect is then seen retrieving keys from a lock box, before entering the van and fleeing the area.     I then spoke with the customer. He stated that he had dropped the vehicle off at the listed location at approximately 18:40 hours. He further stated that upon dropping it off, he was greeted by the suspect, who acted as an employee of the business. The customer reported that after a brief discussion, he was wary of the suspect and secured the keys in the lock box himself. He further stated that he then left the area.       A check in house of our tow list was conducted, with the vehicle not having been reported as towed or repossessed.  The complainant was advised of the recovery process, including fingerprinting of the vehicle. The complainant stated that she would like the vehicle held for prints on behalf of U-Haul and signed a Consent to Search Form. The complainant advised that she was unsure if the vehicle had a tracking system.       The vehicle is valued at approximately $45,000.00.            The complainant has video footage of the incident, available upon request.       Vehicle was entered into the ATTF website.    A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed.Insurance Info: Self-InsuredCertificate #:S012Exp: 06/30/2025

Narrative: On Wednesday, February 5, 2025 at approximately 0300 hours,  P.O Dominguez and I responded to Hackensack Pharmacy (441 Passaic Street) on a reported activated burglar alarm.   Upon my arrival, I observed the main entrance glass door located on the north side of the business to be shattered.  I also observed a master lock and a metal pin on the ground near the door that appeared to had been cut off the steel roller gate located in front of the glass door. I also observed a white prescription pill bottle on the ground near the master lock.  I advised police communications of my findings and P.O A. Lopez , P.O N. Marza and P.O Mattalian arrived to assist.  A perimeter of the business was immediately established.  P.O Dominguez, P.O N. Marza and I made entry into the business and completed an interior check.  The interior check yielded negative results for any person(s) in the business.  While completing the interior check, I observed two gray large paver stones on the floor near the front glass door.  It appears the two stones were used to break the glass to the front door to gain access.  I observed several white prescription pill bottles on the floor as well as several pairs of eye glasses.  Upon further inspection, I observed a white wooden cabinet located behind the service counter that appeared to have been broken into which contained several white prescription pill bottles similar to the prescription pill bottle located outside on the ground in front of the business.  P.O Dominguez made notification to the tour commander. At approximately 0445 hours, Det. Scarpa and the business owner, Mr. Bodawala, arrived and were updated.  Mr. Bodawala consented to the business to be processed and signed a Consent to Search form.  Mr. Bodawala showed video footage of the incident to Det. Scarpa and e-mailed the footage to Det. Scarpa.  See Det. Scarpa's Supplemental report for further. At 0315 hours, the scene was preserved and a crime log had commenced.-0448 hours, Det. N. LaSala (BCSD-CSU) arrived to process the scene, BCSD case # 2025-0088.-0528 hours, Det. N. LaSala completed processing the scene. The scene was turned over to Mr. Bodawala. Mr. Bodawala advised it is unknown what proceeds/value were taken at this time. He advised once he and his employee conduct and complete an inventory, he will compile a list and forward it to Det. Scarpa. Case turned over to Det. Scarpa.
Narrative - Copy: On Wednesday, February 5, 2025 at approximately 0300 hours,  P.O Dominguez and I responded to Hackensack Pharmacy (441 Passaic Street) on a reported activated burglar alarm.   Upon my arrival, I observed the main entrance glass door located on the north side of the business to be shattered.  I also observed a master lock and a metal pin on the ground near the door that appeared to had been cut off the steel roller gate located in front of the glass door. I also observed a white prescription pill bottle on the ground near the master lock.  I advised police communications of my findings and P.O A. Lopez , P.O N. Marza and P.O Mattalian arrived to assist.  A perimeter of the business was immediately established.  P.O Dominguez, P.O N. Marza and I made entry into the business and completed an interior check.  The interior check yielded negative results for any person(s) in the business.  While completing the interior check, I observed two gray large paver stones on the floor near the front glass door.  It appears the two stones were used to break the glass to the front door to gain access.  I observed several white prescription pill bottles on the floor as well as several pairs of eye glasses.  Upon further inspection, I observed a white wooden cabinet located behind the service counter that appeared to have been broken into which contained several white prescription pill bottles similar to the prescription pill bottle located outside on the ground in front of the business.  P.O Dominguez made notification to the tour commander. At approximately 0445 hours, Det. Scarpa and the business owner, Mr. Bodawala, arrived and were updated.  Mr. Bodawala consented to the business to be processed and signed a Consent to Search form.  Mr. Bodawala showed video footage of the incident to Det. Scarpa and e-mailed the footage to Det. Scarpa.  See Det. Scarpa's Supplemental report for further. At 0315 hours, the scene was preserved and a crime log had commenced.-0448 hours, Det. N. LaSala (BCSD-CSU) arrived to process the scene, BCSD case # 2025-0088.-0528 hours, Det. N. LaSala completed processing the scene. The scene was turned over to Mr. Bodawala. Mr. Bodawala advised it is unknown what proceeds/value were taken at this time. He advised once he and his employee conduct and complete an inventory, he will compile a list and forward it to Det. Scarpa. Case turned over to Det. Scarpa.

Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I met with the complainant and the victim. The victim only spoke Spanish, so her husband (the complainant) translated. The complainant stated that his wife's vehicle was parking in the driveway of 170 Hudson Street, which was located between 170 Hudson Street and 166 Hudson Street. He stated that he left his vehicle running and went back into his house at 0635 hours. When he went to check on the vehicle at 0650 hours, he discovered it was missing. The vehicle was left unlocked with the keys in the ignition. I observed cameras in the rear of the house, but none located directly where the vehicle was parked.A victim notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim. The victim requested to have the vehicle process if located. The estimated value of the stolen auto is $2,000.00 Dollars.

Narrative: The victim responded to police headquarters on a report of a motor vehicle theft.  The victim, Clever Asitimbay Mora, reported that he parked the vehicle in the area of 535 Hudson Street in this jurisdiction on 01/31/2025.  The victim reported that on 02/7/2025 at approximately 13:00 hours, he checked the GPS location of his vehicle on his phone and noticed that his vehicle was located at 3945 Frankford Avenue, Philadelphia, PA 19124.  He reported he possessed one (1) key to the vehicle, and the other key was broken.  Both the front and rear license plates were attached to the vehicle.  The victim did not want the vehicle processed if located.  The vehicle is insured by GEICO unknown policy number at this time.  The value of the vehicle is approximately $6,000.00.  A victim notification form was completed, and the vehicle was entered into N.C.I.C and the Auto Theft Task Force (ATTF) as stolen.  I contacted Philadelphia Police Department District 24 and spoke with P.O. Kane and advised him of the location of the vehicle.

Narrative: On Tuesday, 01/14/2025, P.O. Rivera and I responded to 895 Main Street apartment 12A for a Domestic Violence incident report. Mrs. Charmaine Perez (victim) is the mother of Mr. Christopher Pereira (suspect). Mr. Eusebia Perez (witness) is married to the victim and stepfather to the suspect.      The victim reported that the suspect has not lived at the apartment for approximately three (3) months. The suspect does not have key access to the apartment. There is a vestibule, which is kept locked, that leads to a first and second floor apartment. The victim's apartment is on the first floor.       The victim reported that the suspect broke into the apartment uninvited and continued into her bedroom, where she was located. The suspect was irate, confronted her about money that she was awarded from a lawsuit settlement. The suspect stated, " BITCH, DON'T MESS WITH THE GRANDKIDS, DO YOU KNOW WHO THAT NIGGA IS ON THE COUCH, STAY THE FUCK AWAY FROM ME!" The victim reported at the time her granddaughter, Ms. Keka Ornsby, and her boyfriend were seated on the couch when the suspect entered. The victim reported that the suspect did not engage with Ms. Keka Ornsby. It should be noted, Ms. Ornsby's mother has an active temporary restraining order (Docket #FV-02000101-11 against the suspect. Ms. Ornsby is listed as an additional person in the order. The victim reported that Ms. Ornsby began staying with her for approximately three (3) weeks. Due to the statements the victim made, we did not sign a complaint against suspect for 2C:29-9 (Contempt of court).       The victim reported when the suspect exited the bedroom, the granddaughter and her boyfriend left and the witness entered the apartment. The witness reported that he avoided confrontation from the suspect by exiting the apartment. The witness was unaware the suspect was inside and the victim confirmed his reporting. It should be noted, the suspect has a no contact order against the witness (See complaint #W2024-000767-0223). Due to the victim's and witness's statement, we did not sign a complaint against the witness for 2C:29-9 (Contempt of court).      Sgt. Williams arrived to assist. The victim believed the suspect used a credit card to gain entry into the apartment. The vestibule door and apartment door had old damage near the doorknob and strike plate. The victim confirmed our observation. Sgt. Detective Bay was notified. The scene was unable to be processed, but was captured by my body worn camera.      The victim wanted a temporary restraining order and willing to give a recorded statement. She was transported to headquarters, gave a recorded statement to detectives (See their supplement), and Judge Takvorian granted the TRO in report room #2 @1342 hours. Sgt. Feuilly was sent an e-mail to preserve the recording. The victim was given her copy of the TRO. She did not want to speak with a Domestic Violence Response member, but was provided their phone number. A voluntary handwritten form was not completed because she gave a recorded statement. A New Jersey Victim notification form was completed.      Lt. Knapp authored complaint #W-0223-2025-000014 for 2C:33-4C (Harassment), a petty disorderly persons offense, and 2C:18-2A(1) (Burglary), a third degree crime.

All_Incident_Types: Violation of Court Order, Burglary - Residence, Harassment
Narrative: The victim and the suspect were prviously in a two year relationship.  The two never resided together and have no children in common.     P.O. McCarthy and I responded to the listed location on a report of a violation of a court order.  Upon arrival, we met with the victim,  Johnathan Arroyo, who reported hearing a loud bang from the basement door.  After hearing the loud bang, he began to hear his ex-girlfriend, Ashley Celdo Rodriguez, start to scream while standing in the basement.  That's when his brother, David Arroyo, and the victim exit their bedrooms and witness the suspect inside of the house.  Both parties grabbed the suspect and escorted her out of the house.  Once outside, the suspect ran toward the backyard fence and climbed over before our arrival on the scene.  While on the scene, I observed the door frame trim to be damaged.  The victim estimated that the cost to fix the door was $200.00.  P.O. Mcarthy and P.O. Lopez-Amaya arrived to assist and conducted a check of the area.     While on the scene, I observed several surveillance cameras on the residence's exterior.  They showed me the footage of the suspect entering the rear yard and walking to the basement door of the house.  I provided the victim with my departmental e-mail address so that the video footage could be sent.        I contacted police headquarters to see if any active restraining orders were in place.  They advised me that three no-contact orders were in place.  I asked if the victim wanted the scene processed, but he did not want it done.  I asked the victim if he wanted to apply for a restraining order at this time, which he denied.  He also wanted to speak with a DVRT member at a later time.  I provided the victim with the telephone number.  I completed the New Jersey victim notification form and domestic violence supplement were completed.   The victim refused to complete a voluntary handwritten statement.  While at police headquarters, P.O. McCarthy, P.O. Vicale, and P.O. Lopez-Amaya located the suspect. See their supplements for further information.       Arrestee was charged with 2C:29-17-3A(1), 2C:33-4A, 2C:18-2A(1), 2C:29-2A(1) and 3 counts of 2C:29-9A(1) on 0223-W-2025-000023

FullAddress: East Pleasantview Avenue & Kipp Street, Hackensack, NJ, 07601
Narrative: I responded to East Pleasantview and Kipp St. on a burglary to auto. Upon arrival I met with the complainant, Mr. Santangelo who is an employee of PSE&G. He stated that he parked and locked his work vehicle (NJ-XGDR55) on 2/13/25 at approximately 1600 hours then returned on 2/14/25 at 0710 hours and noticed the rear passenger side window was completely shattered.     The window was shattered and the unknown suspect(s) were able to unlock the door from the inside and enter the vehicle. Mr. Santangelo reported numerous tools were stolen. Mr. Santangelo touched the inside of the vehicle prior to my arrival, contaminating it making it unable to be processed. Stolen tools:1-Metro Tech 810 Mark out scope- approximately $3,000.001-Heath RMLD- Approximately $8,000.001-Heath gas detector- Approximately $5,000.00                                            Approximate amount stolen: $16,000.00    I did not observe any cameras in the area and the PSE&G vehicle is self insured.

All_Incident_Types: Motor Vehicle Theft, Recovered Stolen Vehicle
Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown     The victim, Kimberly Trujillo, responded to Police Headquarters on a report of a Motor Vehicle Theft.  Upon arrival, the victim stated that her 2022 grey 4-door Honda Accord bearing NJ reg: L17PUM had been stolen from her apartment complex's parking lot and had been located in Rochelle Park. The victim further stated the listed vehicle was parked in assigned space number #2 and was last known to be parked there at approximately 2300 hours. The victim advised me she was in possession of the master key and no other party had access to her vehicle. The victim further advised me the spare key to her vehicle was misplaced approximately two (2) years in the past. Both license plates were attached to the vehicle at the time the victim parked the vehicle.     The victim stated on 02/17/2025 at approximately 0800, she received a phone call from Rochelle Park Police Department Det. Galina #151 advising her the listed vehicle had been located behind a CrossFit gym in Rochelle Park. Det. Gallina #151 further advised the victim, that the listed vehicle was located with all four (4) wheels removed from the vehicle. The victim further stated upon receiving the phone call, they responded to their second vehicle which was also parked in their apartment complex's parking lot. At this time, the victim observed an E-Z Pass and Camera from the stolen vehicle left on the hood of her second vehicle.      The victim stated she would like to have the vehicle processed should it be recovered and signed a consent to search form. A victim notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim. The vehicle's insurance information was unable to be provided due to the paperwork being inside the glove box of the stolen vehicle.The vehicle registration was given to dispatch and was entered into NCIC. A stolen vehicle entry was made into the Auto Theft Task Force website.The vehicle's estimated value is approximately $22,000 USD.  See Rochelle Park Case #2025-002492.

Narrative: On February 17, 2025 I responded to 300 Atlantic Street (HUMC Parking Garage) on a report of a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who is the registered owner of the vehicle and his wife. The victim advised me that his wife parked the vehicle (2020 Honda Accord blue) inside the monthly permit parking section near the entrance of the garage at approximately 2000 hours last night. This morning around 0900 hours they arrived to the garage and discovered that the vehicle was missing. They advised me that they only have one key and checked the area for the vehicle but were met with negative results. I advised Headquarters to check if the vehicle was towed from the premises which also yielded negative results. At this time I advised the registered owner the procedure with reporting a stolen vehicle. At this time he stated he wanted the vehicle processed and signed a consent to search form. A victim notification form was also completed and a copy was provided to the victim. Upon completion the vehicle was placed into NCIC and ATTF as stolen.

Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown     I responded to the listed location, 35 Pangborn Place - Hackensack Emergency Dental, on a report of a burglary. Upon arrival, I was met by the complainant, Beau Jin, who is the manager of the business. The complainant stated that a TP-Link 300 Mbps Wireless Router ($100.00 USD) and a Keep Connect Router Reporter ($50.00) had been a taken from the basement. The complainant further stated there did not appear to be any forced entry into the basement. I was then advised by the complainant that the basement was accessible by the employees as well as the residence of the apartments on the second and third floor.     The complainant advised me the business was closed and secured for on 02/14/2025 until 02/16/2025. The complainant further advised me he on 02/15/2025 he observed through his phone that the businesses router was inoperable. The complainant stated upon opening business on 02/17/2025 he responded to the basement and observed that the router was taken. The complainant further reported he observed feces on the handle of the main entrance door and in the amazon drop off box located in front of the business.      The scene was unable to be processed due to the complainant contamination the scene as well as the fecal matter contaminating the door handle.Sgt. Farhi was notified. Detectives were notified by Sgt. Farhi. Total value of items taken - $150.00 USD
